'''Wayne Robert Godwin''' (born 13 March 1982) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|hk}} in the 2000s and 2010s.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

Godwin played for [[Castleford Tigers]], [[Wigan Warriors]], [[Hull FC]], [[Bradford Bulls]] and [[Salford Red Devils]] in the [[Super League]], and for [[Dewsbury Rams]] in the [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]].

==Background==
Godwin was born in [[Pontefract]], [[West Yorkshire]], [[England]]. He supported the [[Castleford Tigers]] as a child.{{citation needed|date=April 2017}}

==Early career==
Godwin turned pro in 2002, being signed from his local club Knottingley Rockware to play for Castleford until the end of 2004, making over 60 appearances.{{citation needed|date=April 2017}} Godwin signed a two-year deal with Wigan after Castleford's [[relegation]] at the end of 2004's [[Super League IX]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/3737198.stm |title=Godwin completes Wigan move |work=BBC Sport |date=13 October 2004 |access-date=23 July 2024}}</ref>

Goodwin was called into the England A squad for the 2004 [[European Challenge Cup]].{{citation needed|date=April 2017}}

Godwin signed for [[Hull F.C.]] in 2006.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/hull/5307964.stm |title=Hooker Godwin agrees Hull switch |work=BBC Sport |date=2 September 2006 |access-date=23 July 2024}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.wiganwarriors.com/news_full.asp?newsid=2297 |title=Godwin Signs For Hull FC |website=Wigan Warriors |date=2 September 2006 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070930155654/http://www.wiganwarriors.com/news_full.asp?newsid=2297 |archive-date=30 September 2007}}</ref>

===Bradford===
Godwin signed for Bradford in 2008 and was the understudy to [[Terry Newton]], during his time at Bradford he always wore the number 14 shirt and established himself as a fans' favourite.{{citation needed|date=April 2017}} 
During his time at Bradford he played many different positions including loose forward several times.

[[Mick Potter]]'s arrival at Odsal and the arrival of the vastly experienced hooker [[Matt Diskin]] put Godwin in a position where he would be the 3rd choice hooker and would not feature in every game of the 2011 season.{{citation needed|date=April 2017}}

===Salford===
It was announced that Godwin had joined Salford due to their intended hooker, [[Tevita Leo Latu]] being refused a visa.{{citation needed|date=April 2017}} On Godwin's début, he scored a try but Salford still lost 13–12 to Leigh.

===Dewsbury===
Goodwin signed for Dewsbury for the 2014 season, playing in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. On 15 March 2015, Godwin returned on a four-week loan to Salford.{{citation needed|date=April 2017}}

==Outside rugby league==
In 2013, Godwin launched a clothing brand with former team mates [[Jamie Langley]] and [[Duane Straugheir]] named 'We Are Taurus'.<ref>{{cite web|last1=Heppenstall|first1=Ross|title=Bradford Bulls: Jamie Langley hopes 'Beast Mode' is all the rage as he starts new clothing range|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/10330159.Bradford_Bulls__Langley_hopes____Beast_Mode____is_all_the_rage|website=Bradford Telegraph & Argus|access-date=8 January 2018|date=4 April 2013}}</ref>
